concerns.analysis:
The source PDF contains multiple highly meaningful flowchart diagrams illustrating water flow networks (on pages 10 through 16). In the HTML output, these images do not have corresponding `` tags. Instead, they have been replaced with bracketed text placeholders (e.g., `[Image: A flow chart diagram...]`) wrapped in `

` tags. Because the actual image elements are entirely missing from the HTML for these critical diagrams, this constitutes a severe loss of meaningful visual information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Flowchart diagram on page 10 showing the water flow network of major nodes in the Upper Cape Fear Basin.
concerns.output_quote:
[Image: A flow chart diagram showing the water flow network of major nodes in the Upper Cape Fear Basin...]

(No element is present)
concerns.location: Page 10 / Under heading 'Flow Chart of Major Nodes in the Upper Cape Fear Basin'

concerns.analysis: There are two list-related defects on page 2. First, the Table of Contents is retained with its original page number references (rendered as a table instead of removed or converted to links). As specified in the instructions, retaining a PDF TOC with page numbers in the HTML output provides dead navigation and is a major defect. Second, the 'Appendices' list uses non-standard alphanumeric markers ('A', 'B1', 'B2', 'C', 'D'). The extraction pipeline converted this to an 'ordered' list, which will
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Table of Contents
Section Page
1 – Introduction 3
[...]
Appendices
A - Model Static Input Data and Run Code
B1 – Finalized Inflow Data Development – Cape Fear
concerns.location: Page 2 (Table of Contents and Appendices list)

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 9.0x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=9.00. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.
header_footer_count=18. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.
